issues: 1x silhouette mushy — ring body and gem merge at native size
light-bg contrast adequate but not punchy; gold-on-cream lacks pop
pixel clutter around ring band reduces clean read
reasoning: Charm is genuine: warm gold/orange ring with a pink gem-face topper has friendly toy energy and a round compact silhouette that passes the sticker test at 4x. Colors are inviting and the personality reads — small face peeking above the band is cute and legible at 4x on both dark and light panels. However at 1x the gem and ring top-edge merge into an indistinct blob; the silhouette collapses rather than simplifying. On the light background the gold band sits against cream without strong edge definition, so the outer contour needs either a darker outline pixel or stronger value contrast. Not charmless and not unreadable, but not clear enough at native resolution to fully pass — sits at the top of marginal.
